title: WOS Provenance Log
description: Append-only audit log produced by WOS runtime execution. Per ADR 0063 §2.3 this is a runtime artifact (not author-time), emitted by processors, never hand-authored. The required `$wosProvenanceLog` marker gives runtime artifacts the same identity discipline as author-time documents and lets the lint parser detect them uniformly. Wraps every persisted record kind: state-transition records (Kernel §8), case-file snapshots (Kernel §8 — required when transitionTags includes 'determination'), capability-invocation records (AI Integration §3.3.1), signature-affirmation records (Signature Profile §2.8), case-state mutation records carrying mutationSource / verificationLevel (Kernel §5.4, ADR 0080), iteration records (Kernel §4.10/foreach), and verification certificates (formerly the runtime half of `wos-verification-report`). Facts-tier semantics are normative in the kernel and live in the merged `wos-workflow.schema.json` $defs (ADR 0076 step 5 promotion: FactsTierRecord, MutationSource, VerificationLevel, CaseFileSnapshot, CapabilityInvocationRecord). Higher tiers reuse the same envelope with `auditLayer` set accordingly. This schema validates the post-append export shape -- not unstamped in-memory constructors.

$wosProvenanceLog.description: Top-level marker identifying this object as a WOS Provenance Log runtime artifact, pinned to the 1.0 line. Required per ADR 0063 §2.3 so the lint parser detects runtime artifacts uniformly with author-time documents.

provenanceLog.description: Ordered sequence of persisted provenance records emitted during a workflow run. Records are append-only and their order matches emission order; consumers MUST preserve ordering when re-exporting.

CaseCreatedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records that mark the governed-case boundary. The kernel reserves the `caseCreated` kind and the canonical event literal `wos.kernel.case_created`, and it requires the created governed-case reference in `data.caseRef` plus the durable case-ledger TypeID in `data.caseLedgerId`. The kernel does not freeze the rest of the binding-specific `data` keys; bindings may carry different evidence references inside `data` as long as the record still identifies the created governed case.

IntakeAcceptedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when runtime intake acceptance succeeds. Accepted intake creates or updates governed-case handling, so the record MUST carry the canonical event literal `wos.kernel.intake_accepted`, binding/intake context in `data`, the accepted case disposition, the canonical governed-case reference, and at least one governed-case output reference.

IntakeRejectedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when runtime intake acceptance rejects a handoff. Rejection records remain detached from governed case mutation and therefore do not require governed-case outputs.

IntakeDeferredRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when runtime intake acceptance defers a handoff. Deferred records preserve intake context and reason code without creating or updating governed case state.

CaseCreatedData.description: Kernel-owned minimum payload for a governed-case boundary record. `caseRef` identifies the created governed case and `caseLedgerId` pins the durable case-ledger TypeID; bindings may add their own evidence references alongside it.

caseRef.description: Governed-case reference created or acknowledged by the boundary event.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable case-ledger TypeID created or acknowledged by the boundary event.

IntakeDecisionData.description: Minimum runtime-owned payload for non-accepted intake decision provenance. The kernel fixes the intake identity, binding discriminator, and case intent fields so exports and offline verifiers can trace the rejection or deferral back to a concrete handoff. Bindings or host profiles may add more fields.

binding.description: Binding discriminator that interpreted the handoff, such as `formspec`.

intakeId.description: Stable intake identity used for replay and provenance correlation.

caseIntent.description: Binding-derived intent before host policy chose attach, create, reject, or defer.

code.description: Machine-readable rejection or deferral code when intake was not accepted.

IntakeAcceptedData.description: Kernel-owned minimum payload for an accepted intake decision. Accepted intake records MUST identify the runtime's final case disposition, canonical governed-case reference, and durable case-ledger TypeID so exports and offline verifiers can bind the intake handoff to the governed case that was created or attached.

caseDisposition.description: Final accepted case disposition when the runtime accepted the handoff.

caseRef.description: Governed-case reference selected by the runtime when acceptance attaches or creates a case.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable case-ledger TypeID selected by the runtime when acceptance attaches or creates a case.

definitionUrl.description: Kernel definition URL used when acceptance created a governed case.

definitionVersion.description: Kernel definition version used when acceptance created a governed case.

ForEachIterationStartedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for ForEach iteration-start records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

ForEachIterationCompletedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for ForEach iteration-completion records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

ForEachCompletedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for ForEach completion records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

SignatureAffirmationRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted by the Signature Profile. A `wos.kernel.signature_affirmation` event MUST carry the signer, role, document, source signature id, signed-payload digest, signing intent, timestamp, identity-binding, consent, provider, ceremony, profile, source response/evidence reference, custody eligibility, and primitive-verification status fields required by Signature Profile §2.8. The event literal is the authoritative D26 dispatch key.

event.description: F-13 custody/export event literal that authoritatively selects SignatureAffirmation record shape during D26 migration.

SignatureAdmissionFailedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when a signature is evaluated but not admitted into workflow state (ADR-0089). A `wos.kernel.signature_admission_failed` event MUST carry the evidence bindings, reason, and emission timestamp. The event literal is the authoritative D26 dispatch key.

event.description: F-13 custody/export event literal that authoritatively selects SignatureAdmissionFailed record shape during D26 migration.

DeterminationRescindedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for determination rescission records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

ReinstatedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for reinstatement records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

ClockStartedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for statutory-clock start records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

ClockResolvedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for statutory-clock resolution records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

IdentityAttestationEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for identity-attestation records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

KeyRebindEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for key-rebind recovery records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

KeyRebindRecord.description: Payload shape for key-rebind recovery records, promoted from the workflow schema so provenance-log exports validate the recovery ceremony fields.

StateTransitionEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for state-transition records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key while data.transitionEvent preserves the workflow trigger.

transitionEvent.description: Kernel workflow event that triggered the transition; distinct from the canonical D26 record event literal.

CapabilityInvocationEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for capability-invocation records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

CorrectionAuthorizedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for correction-authorization records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

AmendmentAuthorizedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for amendment-authorization records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

DeterminationAmendedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for determination-amended records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

RescissionAuthorizedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for rescission-authorization records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

AuthorizationAttestationEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for authorization-attestation records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

ClockSkewObservedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for clock-skew observation records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

CommitAttemptFailureEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for commit-attempt failure records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

AuthorizationRejectedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for authorization-rejection records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

MigrationPinChangedEventRecord.description: D26 event-literal guard layered on provenance-log exports for migration-pin-change records. Runtime exports use the canonical F-13 event literal as the authoritative dispatch key.

SignatureAffirmationData.description: Required payload for a Signature Profile affirmation provenance record. Source signature identity and signed-payload digest fields identify the verified signature evidence that WOS accepted; identity-binding and consent-reference shapes remain provider-neutral so Formspec and external ceremony adapters can map into the same WOS evidence record.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable case-ledger TypeID this workflow-emitted decision payload appends into. Required by the F-11 workflow profile identity placement rule.

processId.description: Workflow runtime process TypeID that emitted this decision payload. Present for workflow-emitted decisions and omitted for direct case-ledger appends.

signerId.description: Stable signer identifier from the ceremony context or assigned WOS actor.

roleId.description: Signature Profile role id that was satisfied by this accepted signing act.

role.description: Signature Profile role literal that defines the signer ceremony semantics.

documentId.description: Signature Profile document id whose declared digest was affirmed by the signer.

signingActId.description: Opaque signing-act identifier shared by all SignatureAffirmation records produced by one signer action, including localized document variants.

documentRef.description: Structured reference to the rendered document presentation the signer saw. K-2 requires documentId plus locale so multiple localized affirmations can share a signingActId without losing presentation identity.

documentId.description: Signature Profile document id for the rendered presentation.

locale.description: BCP 47 locale token for the rendered presentation, or `und` when no locale was declared.

documentHash.description: Digest of the document bytes that the signer affirmed during the ceremony.

presentationHash.description: Digest of the rendered presentation bytes under `trellis-presentation-artifact-v1` that the signer saw.

documentHashAlgorithm.description: Digest algorithm used to compute documentHash for the affirmed document.

sourceSignatureSystem.description: Binding or provider family that supplied the verified signature evidence.

sourceSignatureId.description: Source-system signature identifier accepted into this WOS governance affirmation.

signedPayloadDigest.description: Source signature evidence digest for the signer-assented payload. This is distinct from any transferred response-envelope hash.

signedPayloadDigestAlgorithm.description: Digest algorithm used to compute signedPayloadDigest for the signer-assented source payload.

signingIntent.description: WOS signing-intent URI accepted for this affirmation; it MUST equal the consumed source signature evidence intent.

signedAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the signer accepted the signing act and consent.

signatureProvider.description: Provider or adapter that supplied the signing ceremony evidence.

ceremonyId.description: Provider or adapter ceremony identifier for the accepted signing session.

profileRef.description: URI reference to the Signature Profile used for this signing act.

profileKey.description: Package-local Signature Profile key used for this signing act.

sourceResponseRef.description: URI reference to the canonical source response or evidence artifact that supplied signature evidence.

custodyHookEligible.description: SignatureAffirmation records are eligible for WOS custodyHook admission.

primitiveVerification.description: Cryptographic primitive-verification status from the binding's authored-signature parser. `verified` requires that the cryptographic signature primitive (canonical-digest + signature-suite check over the binding's signature envelope, e.g. Formspec `signatureValue` — a COSE_Sign1 envelope whose protected-header `method_uri` selects the cryptographic adapter per ADR 0109) executed and passed; `deferredPendingHelper` indicates the parser ran pin/consent/digest pre-checks but did not run the cryptographic primitive (e.g. Formspec signing helper not yet shipped). Failed outcomes are now recorded as separate `signatureAdmissionFailed` records per ADR-0089. A future deployment posture MAY require `verified`; until then both `verified` and `deferredPendingHelper` are admissible.

status.description: Primitive-verification outcome reported by the binding adapter that produced this affirmation; closed enum. 'failed' outcomes are now recorded as separate `signatureAdmissionFailed` records per ADR-0089.

reason.description: Stable machine-readable reason identifier; required when status is `deferredPendingHelper`.

verificationReceipt.description: Optional base64-encoded COSE_Sign1 VerificationReceipt bytes. Present when primitiveVerification.status is 'verified' and a verifier ran. Absent when status is 'deferredPendingHelper'.

witnessedSignatureRef.description: Reference edge to the witnessed signature when this affirmation is a witness/counter-signature; null when the signing act is not witnessing another signature.

SignatureAdmissionFailedData.description: Payload for signatureAdmissionFailed provenance records (ADR-0089). Emitted when a signature is evaluated but not admitted into workflow state.

caseLedgerId.description: Durable case-ledger TypeID this workflow-emitted decision payload appends into. Required by the F-11 workflow profile identity placement rule.

processId.description: Workflow runtime process TypeID that emitted this decision payload. Present for workflow-emitted decisions and omitted for direct case-ledger appends.

reason.description: Closed admission-failure reason explaining why evaluated signature evidence was not accepted into workflow state.

receipt.description: Optional VerificationReceipt object captured when a verifier ran and produced failed or unsupported verification evidence.

responseId.description: Source Formspec response identifier whose authored signature evidence was evaluated for admission.

signedPayloadDigest.description: Hex digest of the source signed payload that the failed admission decision evaluated.

signatureId.description: Source-system signature identifier for the evaluated signature evidence record.

signingIntent.description: Signing-intent URI asserted by the source signature evidence during admission evaluation.

failureContext.description: Reason-specific structured diagnostic data captured for the failed signature-admission decision.

signerAuthority.description: Optional signer-authority evidence observed while evaluating the rejected signature admission.

signerId.description: Optional source signer identifier associated with the rejected signature evidence.

emittedAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the signature-admission failure decision was emitted by runtime.

SignatureSignerAuthority.description: WOS signer-authority claim per Signature Profile §2.12. Carries capacity-to-bind evidence: class, optional delegation principal, optional authoritySource URI, optional evidenceBinding (digest of the supporting commission / power-of-attorney / appointment instrument), and optional validity window. Class enum is closed at the WOS center; deployments add `x-*` classes through the Posture Declaration. Per-class field requirements are enforced via the conditional `allOf` block.

class.description: Signer-authority class accepted by the WOS Signature Profile (§2.12.1). Closed enum (`self`, `witness`, `notary-commissioned`, `as-attorney-in-fact`, `as-officer-of`) plus deployment-declared `x-*` classes.

principal.description: URI of the principal whose interest the signer binds (§2.12.2). REQUIRED for `as-officer-of` and `as-attorney-in-fact` (delegating classes); OPTIONAL for `notary-commissioned`/`witness` (the affiant's URI when present); MUST NOT equal `signerId` for delegating classes — runtime rejects self-delegation.

authoritySource.description: URI of the credential, commission, or appointment instrument that supports the authority claim (§2.12.2). REQUIRED for `as-officer-of`, `as-attorney-in-fact`, and `notary-commissioned`; OPTIONAL for `self`/`witness`.

evidenceBinding.description: Cryptographic binding from the authority claim to its proof (§2.12.3). `evidenceHash` is the digest of the canonical supporting evidence (commission certificate bytes, executed power-of-attorney bytes, board-resolution bytes); `sha-256` is REQUIRED for Core conformance. `evidenceLocation` is an optional URI for retrieval — trust derives from the hash, not from URI resolvability.

evidenceHash.description: Digest of the canonical supporting evidence bytes (§2.12.3). Hex-encoded; length matches the algorithm.

evidenceHashAlgorithm.description: Digest algorithm for `evidenceHash`. `sha-256` is REQUIRED for Core conformance per §2.7.

evidenceLocation.description: Optional URI to retrieve the supporting evidence; MAY be a `canonical_event_hash` URI when the evidence is registered as its own ledger event. Retrieval-only — trust derives from `evidenceHash`.

validFrom.description: RFC 3339 date-time at which the authority became effective (§2.12.1). Optional; when present, runtime requires `signedAt >= validFrom`.

validUntil.description: RFC 3339 date-time at which the authority expires (§2.12.1). Optional; when present, runtime requires `signedAt <= validUntil`.

extensions.description: Open extension bag for vendor or deployment-defined authority metadata (§2.12.1). Inner keys MUST follow the `x-*` extension discipline.

SignatureIdentityBinding.description: Provider-neutral identity-binding evidence for a signature affirmation.

method.description: Authentication method used to bind the signer identity to the signing act.

assuranceLevel.description: Assurance strength reached by the identity-binding evidence.

providerRef.description: URI reference to the identity or signature provider, when used.

externalAttestationRef.description: URI reference to an external identity attestation, when one exists.

SignatureConsentReference.description: Consent text/version and response evidence paths used to prove signing intent.

consentTextRef.description: URI reference to the consent text accepted by the signer during the ceremony.

consentVersion.description: Version of the consent text accepted by the signer and recorded as evidence.

acceptedAtPath.description: Path to timestamp evidence in the Formspec response or case file.

affirmationPath.description: Path to the explicit affirmation evidence that proves signing intent.
